title: Out-of-Distribution Detection and Selective Generation for Conditional Language Models

structured.summary: The paper introduces a method for detecting out-of-distribution (OOD) inputs in conditional language models (CLMs) using embeddings, outperforming baseline methods in tasks like summarization and translation. It emphasizes the ineffectiveness of perplexity in OOD detection and suggests the use of Mahalanobis distances and binary classifiers for better accuracy in generating high-quality outputs.

strengths.point: Lightweight and accurate OOD detection method.
strengths.evidence: The paper proposes using embeddings of input and output data for OOD detection without needing a separate model.
The method is shown to improve the reliability of CLMs in user-facing tasks like text summarization and translation.

weaknesses.evidence: The use of Mahalanobis distances and binary classifiers builds on existing concepts rather than introducing substantially new techniques.
The results section lacks specific quantitative data on how the proposed method compares against baselines.
Details on the parameterization of embeddings and their processing are not well elaborated, making replication difficult.

questions.question: How does the method perform across different domains and tasks beyond summarization and translation?
questions.motivation: Understanding the generalizability of the method is crucial for its application across various real-world tasks.

questions.question: What specific improvements in accuracy and reliability does the method demonstrate over baseline techniques?
questions.motivation: Quantitative comparisons are key to evaluating the true impact of the proposed methodology.

critique_points.point_001: Lack of novelty in approach.. The use of Mahalanobis distances and binary classifiers builds on existing concepts rather than introducing substantially new techniques
critique_points.point_002: Insufficient evaluation metrics and comparisons.. The results section lacks specific quantitative data on how the proposed method compares against baselines
critique_points.point_003: Limited methodological detail.. Details on the parameterization of embeddings and their processing are not well elaborated, making replication difficult
